statement.markdown: Given an array of integers, find the first duplicate number. The array is sorted in ascending order.
statement.inputFormat: The first line of input contains an integer n, the size of the array. The next line contains n space-separated integers.
statement.notes: If no duplicates are found, print -1.

hints.text: Check the array for consecutive numbers.

editorial.title: Editorial: Missing Duplicate Finder
editorial.intuition: The problem requires us to find the first duplicate number in an array of integers.
editorial.bruteForce: One approach is to iterate through the array and check for each number if it has occurred before.
editorial.optimalSolution: We can use a HashSet to keep track of the numbers we have seen so far. If we encounter a number that is already in the HashSet, it's a duplicate.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The optimal solution has a time complexity of O(n) and a space complexity of O(n).
commonMistakes: Not handling the edge case where the array is empty.
Not checking if the array is sorted.
